verification.target_vocab_notes: 病気 appears clearly in the sentence
verification.kana_notes: Kana reading matches the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'ので' (because/since) which is an N4 causal conjunction. Also uses 'ときいた' (heard that) which involves reported speech, also N4.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses complex causal reasoning with ので and reported hearsay pattern.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with ので causal conjunction and reported speech pattern

verification.target_vocab_notes: ポケット appears clearly at sentence start
verification.grammar_notes: Uses に particle for location, を particle, question word 何, ています form for state (possessing), question marker か. The ています here is borderline but acceptable for possession state in N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses question structure, location particle, and ています for state. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with clear target vocabulary

verification.target_vocab_notes: ポケット appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'ていた' which is past progressive form. This is N4 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses past progressive form which exceeds N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with past progressive ていた form

verification.target_vocab_notes: ポケット appears as part of compound ポケット型 (pocket-sized)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, noun modification with の, and copula だ. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses demonstrative これ, compound noun formation, and basic copula. Standard N5 level.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with appropriate grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: Target is 頭 (atama - head), but this word does NOT appear in the sentence at all. The sentence uses しったかぶり which is a different word.
verification.kana_notes: Kana reading matches the sentence, but target vocab is missing
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, でも particle combination (何でも - anything/everything), and basic verb する. Grammar is N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5 if target were present. Uses 何でも construction and idiomatic expression.
verification.overall_notes: Target vocabulary 頭 does not appear in the sentence at all. Complete mismatch.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 頭 appears in the idiomatic expression 頭にくる (to get angry)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle and に particle with くる verb. All N5 grammar, though the idiom is figurative.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses idiomatic expression 頭にくる which is figurative rather than literal use of 'head'. May be confusing for beginners learning the literal word.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is N5 but uses highly idiomatic expression. Questionable for teaching the literal meaning of 頭 (head).

verification.target_vocab_notes: 頭 appears as subject of the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses が particle, i-adjective 重い, and explanatory のです. While のです is sometimes considered N4, basic usage is often included in late N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses explanatory のです pattern and abstract feeling expression. Borderline complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with literal use of 頭. The のです is borderline but acceptable.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 英語 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'べきだ' which is an N4 grammar pattern expressing strong obligation/recommendation (ought to/should).
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses べきだ obligation pattern which exceeds N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with べきだ obligation form

verification.target_vocab_notes: 英語 appears clearly in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses 誰か (someone), を particle, verb 話す, noun modifying verb 人, は particle, and います. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses relative clause structure (英語を話す人) which requires understanding of noun modification, but still within N5 scope.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with appropriate grammar level

verification.target_vocab_notes: 英語 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses adverb 流暢に, を particle, たい desire form, and ものだ pattern. While ものだ can be N4 for expressing general truths, this usage expressing wish/desire is sometimes included in late N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5/borderline N4. Uses advanced adverb 流暢に (fluently) and ものだ pattern for wish expression. Complex vocabulary.
verification.overall_notes: Borderline case. ものだ for wishes and advanced vocabulary 流暢 push toward N4, but could be late N5.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 家 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'なら' conditional particle which is N4 grammar. Also uses complex spatial expression 一軒おいて隣 which is advanced.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses conditional なら and complex spatial description.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with なら conditional

verification.target_vocab_notes: 家 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, time expression 毎晩, に particle for location, and います. All basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple location statement with time expression. Very straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 家 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses exclamatory 何と (how/what a), i-adjective 大きい, を particle, and ている form for possession. The んだろう is explanatory/wondering, which is borderline but the basic structure is N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses exclamatory construction 何と~んだろう which is complex. The wondering/explanatory tone adds difficulty.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is borderline with んだろう wondering pattern. May be too complex for basic N5.

verification.target_vocab_notes: Target is 家 (ie - house), but the sentence uses いえない which is the negative potential form of 言う (iu - to say), meaning 'cannot say'. This is a different word entirely.
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches the sentence but target vocab not present
verification.grammar_notes: Contains multiple N4 patterns: (1) embedded question clause with のか, (2) potential negative form いえない (cannot say), (3) は topic marker used for contrast. Very complex structure.
verification.difficulty_notes: Far too complex. N4 level with embedded clauses, potential form, and abstract reasoning.
verification.overall_notes: Target vocabulary 家 (house) does not appear. Sentence uses いえない (cannot say) from 言う. Complete mismatch. Also N4 grammar.

verification.target_vocab_notes: Target is 家 (ie - house), but sentence uses といえば which is a phrase meaning 'speaking of/as for' from the verb 言う (iu - to say), not 家 (house).
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches but target vocab not present
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'といえば' which is N4 grammar for topic transition (speaking of/as for). Also uses comparative construction with が particle.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses advanced topic transition phrase.
verification.overall_notes: Target vocabulary 家 (house) does not appear. Uses といえば from verb 言う. Complete mismatch plus N4 grammar.

verification.target_vocab_notes: Target is 家 (ie - house), but sentence uses いえない which is the negative potential of 言う (to say), meaning 'cannot say'. Not the same word as 家.
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches but target vocab not present
verification.grammar_notes: Contains potential negative form いえない (cannot say) which is N4. Also uses complex expressions 今のところ and なんとも.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level with potential form and abstract expressions.
verification.overall_notes: Target vocabulary 家 (house) does not appear. Uses いえない from 言う (to say). Complete mismatch plus N4 grammar.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 暑い appears clearly in the sentence
verification.kana_notes: Error: 'こんにちは' should be 'きょうは' (today+は). The reading shows 'konnichi wa' (hello) instead of 'kyou wa' (today).
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, degree adverb とても, and i-adjective 暑い. All basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Very low N5. Simple weather statement with basic adjective and adverb.
verification.overall_notes: Simple N5 grammar but significant kana error. 今日 was read as こんにちは instead of きょう.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 暑い appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, とても adverb, and i-adjective. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Very low N5. Simple weather statement.
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 暑い appears in noun form 暑さ (heat/hotness)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses na-adjective 猛烈な modifying noun 暑さ, and copula だ. All N5 grammar patterns.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses advanced vocabulary 猛烈 (fierce/intense) and nominalized form 暑さ. Vocabulary is challenging.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is N5 but vocabulary 猛烈 is quite advanced. May be too difficult.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 遊ぶ appears in potential negative form 遊べない
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'とっていて' which uses ている progressive form, and '遊べない' which is potential negative form. Potential forms are N4 grammar. Also uses quotation with 言いました.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses potential form, progressive, and reported speech.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with potential form 遊べない (cannot play)

verification.target_vocab_notes: 遊ぶ appears in volitional form 遊びましょう
verification.grammar_notes: Uses ましょう volitional/suggestion form which is basic N5 grammar for suggesting activities together.
verification.difficulty_notes: Very low N5. Simple suggestion sentence. One of the most basic patterns.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example of ましょう suggestion

verification.target_vocab_notes: 遊ぶ appears clearly in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses に particle for direction, dictionary form 遊ぶ, volitional/invitation form 行こう (let's go). All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple invitation sentence with direction particle and volitional form.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with clear target vocabulary

verification.target_vocab_notes: 練習 appears clearly in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses を particle, particle で (means), and masu-form verb します. All basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses instrumental particle で, multiple nouns, and basic verb. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with appropriate grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: 練習 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, time expression 今日, を particle, and masu-form しました. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple past tense statement with time expression. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 最後 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, まで particle (until), past tense 残っていました. The ていました is past progressive/state, which is borderline but the basic structure is acceptable.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses まで particle, past tense with ていた for state. Requires understanding of temporal expressions.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar borderline with ていました past progressive. Could be late N5 or early N4.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 最後 appears with の particle
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative その, の particle, negative form ではない. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses demonstrative, の particle for possession/relation, negative copula. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with clear grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: 最後 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses に particle, te-form with request ください (最後にください - please give last/at the end). Basic N5 pattern.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses time/order expression with に particle and polite request. Standard level.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 閉める appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses を particle, dictionary form 閉める, copula だ. All basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple statement with time expression and basic verb. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 閉める appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses を particle, の nominalizer (閉めるの), を particle again, negative imperative 忘れないで, and ください. While complex, all elements are N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses nominalization with の, embedded verb phrase, negative te-form, and polite request. Multiple layers but still N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example though on the complex side

verification.target_vocab_notes: 閉める appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses を particle, の nominalizer, negative te-form 忘れないで, and sentence-ending particle よ. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses nominalization and negative imperative with よ for emphasis. Standard upper N5 complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: たいへん appears as degree adverb
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, たいへん as degree adverb, another adverb 流暢に, を particle, and dictionary form 話す. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses double adverb modification and advanced vocabulary 流暢 (fluently). Vocabulary challenging but grammar is N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Advanced vocabulary but appropriate grammar.

verification.target_vocab_notes: たいへん appears as degree adverb
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, が particle with 好き, たいへん as adverb, and である (formal copula). While である is more formal, it's included in N5 as a copula variant.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses が好き construction with formal copula である. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with appropriate grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: たいへん appears as degree adverb
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, たいへん adverb, na-adjective 勇敢だ. All basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Simple statement with adverb and na-adjective. Vocabulary 勇敢 (brave) is slightly advanced but manageable.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 作文 appears as subject
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, が particle, na-adjective 優秀だ. Standard N5 X は Y が Adj pattern.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses standard が adjective pattern. Vocabulary 優秀 (excellent) is slightly advanced but the grammar is clear N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 作文 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'かけてます' which is contracted from 'かけています'. More problematically, this uses the potential form 'かける' in progressive, where 書けて means 'can write/is written'. Potential forms are N4.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses potential form in progressive aspect.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with potential form かけて (can write)

verification.target_vocab_notes: 便利 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, na-adjective 便利な modifying もの, and である formal copula. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses na-adjective modification and formal copula. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 便利 appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses そりゃ (contraction of それは), na-adjective 便利だ, and sentence-ending particle ね. All N5 level, though そりゃ is quite casual.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple casual statement. The contraction そりゃ is informal but still N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good casual N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 便利 appears in the sentence
verification.kana_notes: Error: 'かの' should be 'かれの' (his). The kana shows かのいえ but Japanese shows 彼の家 which should read かれのいえ.
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, の possessive, adverb いろいろと, na-adjective 便利だ. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses possessive construction and adverb with na-adjective. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 grammar but kana error present. 彼の should read かれの not かの.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 右 appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses もう少し (a little more), の particle, へ direction particle. All N5 level. The ようし is an interjection.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses direction expression and interjection. Contextual but manageable at N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with directional vocabulary

verification.target_vocab_notes: 右 appears with の particle
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の possessive, を particle, and imperative form 行け (go). While imperative is strong, basic commands are included in N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Direct command sentence. Simple structure.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example of command form

verification.target_vocab_notes: 右 appears in the sentence
verification.kana_notes: Kana reading is accurate. Note: This is a duplicate of example_index 0 with only punctuation difference (— vs ―).
verification.grammar_notes: Same as example 0. Uses もう少し, の, へ particle. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Same as example 0.
verification.overall_notes: Duplicate of example 0, but acceptable N5 grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: 寒い appears in past tense form 寒かった
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'うえに' which is an N4 grammar pattern meaning 'in addition to/moreover/on top of'. This conjunction is beyond N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses N4 conjunction pattern うえに for adding information.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with うえに conjunction pattern

verification.target_vocab_notes: 寒い appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の possessive particle, は particle with では location/context, が particle, and i-adjective です. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses location expression with では, seasonal vocabulary, and standard adjective pattern. Requires understanding of context markers.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with appropriate complexity

verification.target_vocab_notes: 寒い appears in past tense 寒かった
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, degree adverb とても, past tense i-adjective 寒かった. All basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Low N5. Simple past tense weather statement. Very straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 十 appears as part of 十二 (twelve)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses には particle combination, が particle, counter 人, emphasis particle も, and います. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses counter with も emphasis particle, requires understanding of 'as many as' nuance. Standard upper N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with counter usage

verification.target_vocab_notes: 十 appears as part of 十年前 (ten years ago)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, time expression 前に, and past tense 死んだ. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses time expression and past tense with the verb 死ぬ. Straightforward structure.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 十 appears in idiomatic expression 十中八九 (nine times out of ten)
verification.kana_notes: Error: 'じゅっちゅうはっく' should be 'じゅっちゅうはっく' or 'じゅうちゅうはっく'. The reading is missing particles and may have transcription issues. Should be じゅっちゅうはっくくるだろう but shows じゅっちゅうはっくくるだろう.
verification.grammar_notes: Contains だろう plain volitional/probability form which is typically N4. Also uses idiomatic expression 十中八九.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses だろう and idiomatic expression.
verification.overall_notes: N4 grammar with だろう, plus kana error and difficult idiom

verification.target_vocab_notes: 中 appears with に particle
verification.grammar_notes: Contains volitional form 入ろう (let's enter/I'll enter) in plain form. While volitional exists in N5 with ましょう, the plain volitional う/よう form is typically considered N4.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses plain volitional which exceeds basic N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with plain volitional form 入ろう

verification.target_vocab_notes: 中 appears as part of 日中 (all day)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, time expression １日中 (all day), and past tense 吹いた. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses duration expression with 中 suffix and past tense. Standard N5 level.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with time duration vocabulary
